subject: Accounting
title: Footnotes: The Fine Print
description: Learn Footnotes: The Fine Print in one minute.

hook.text: The real story in financial statements often hides in the footnotes nobody reads.

buildup.text: Footnotes disclose accounting methods, risk factors, legal disputes, and related-party deals.

discovery.text: Enron's off-balance-sheet partnerships were buried in footnotes—few investors bothered to look.

twist.text: Companies are legally required to disclose bad news in footnotes. Most readers skip them.

climax.text: Professional analysts read footnotes first. That's where the surprises live.

punchline.text: The footnotes are where companies whisper the truth.

quiz.question: Why are footnotes in financial statements important?
options: They disclose risks, methods, and hidden details
